paper.title: The Role of Aurora Kinase A in HBV-Associated Hepatocellular Carcinomas: A Molecular and Immunohistochemical Study

The Role of Aurora Kinase A in HBV-Associated Hepatocellular Carcinomas: A Molecular and Immunohistochemical Study

evidence_entities.text: AURKA level was significantly increased in both test groups (p < 0.001)
Phospho-P53Ser315 levels were significantly higher in both HBV-HCC and Cr-HCC groups compared to the control group
For phospho-PLK1Thr210, Cr-HCC cases showed statistically higher expression compared to both the control group and HBV-HCC cases
AURKA directly phosphorylates the Thr210 residue within the PLK1 kinase domain, initiating mitotic progression
Overexpression of AURKA has been shown to destabilize p53 through Ser315 phosphorylation
HBx interacts with and binds to p53, thereby suppressing key tumor-suppressive mechanisms

notes: This study focuses on Aurora kinase A (AURKA) and its substrates in hepatocellular carcinoma with different etiologies
The study found distinct patterns of AURKA substrate activation between HBV-associated and cryptogenic HCC
RNA quality issues prevented gene expression analysis, requiring reliance on immunohistochemical methods
No significant copy number variations were detected for AURKA in either HCC subtype
